title: Realm Forces
description: One terse gazetteer line on what defends a realm — for rollable realm entries.
entries: A standing levy of pikemen drilled each new moon; border forts manned in rotation by the county musters.
No standing army by old law; instead a mercenary budget the size of a small war, spent like clockwork.
Knight-service from a hundred manors, splendid and slow; the real work falls to paid crossbow companies.
A river flotilla of shallow-draft galleys; every bridge is fortified and every ferryman is a reservist.
Mountain wardens who fight by avalanche, ambush, and patience; no invader has kept a supply line through the passes in living memory.
A professional army small enough to pay well and drill daily; doctrine holds that three such are worth any horde.
Massed longbow yeomanry, maintained by mandatory Sunday practice; the law against other sports is resented and obeyed.
Clan war-bands under their own banners, terrifying in the charge, ungovernable afterward; the crown's art is aiming them.
A sea wall of hired fleets on retainer; the admirals are foreign, the sailors local, the loyalty seasonal.
Fortress lines built by three dynasties; the garrisons are threadbare, but the stones alone have argued off two invasions.
Horse archers of the high plain; the realm's defense is distance, thirst, and arrows arriving from nowhere.
A temple militia sworn to the border shrines; zealous, free of cost, and alarmingly independent of orders.
City regiments raised by guild — the Dyers' Blue, the Butchers' Red; rivalry on parade, murder in the breach.
War elephants, few and ancient and mostly ceremonial; the drill for facing them, however, no neighbor has.
A ranger service that patrols the wild marches in threes; their reports reach the capital faster than official post.
Conscript infantry by lot, two years' service; grumbling, competent, and the realm's one honest social mixer.
A battle-mage college owing forty days' service a year; the arguing over which forty days is itself a standing institution.
Coastal watchtowers with signal fires tested monthly; the beacon-keepers' families hold their posts like minor titles.
Dwarf-cut tunnels under the border hills, provisioned for sieges that never come; the realm plans to lose the surface and win below.
A famous heavy cavalry, lance and full plate, financed by the horse-tax everyone hates and no one dares repeal.
Free companies licensed against a share of plunder; effective abroad, a headache the moment peace breaks out.
The royal guard alone is professional; the rest is a paper army of enthusiastic rolls and missing boots.
Every adult owes wall-service in their own town; the realm has no field army and has never needed one — a fact its neighbors find maddening.
Griffin riders, a dozen at strength; worth a thousand troops for scouting and exactly twelve in a battle, as the veterans say.
A navy of privateers in peacetime paperwork; the letters of marque are pre-signed and famously undated.
Border legions recruited from pardoned convicts; discipline is savage, morale is strange, results are undeniable.
Militia archery on the village greens, cavalry from the squirearchy, artillery hired by the campaign; a realm that improvises well.
A single strategic pass held by a single great fortress; the realm's entire doctrine is one word — 'hold'.
Longships beached in a hundred coves; the realm defends by raiding first, an approach its diplomats call 'forward'.
An engineer corps prouder of its bridges than its battles; invaders find the roads mined, flooded, or simply gone.
The crown keeps hostage-companies of noble sons from every great house; the army exists mainly so they have somewhere to be.
Wardogs bred to a famous line, issued to every patrol; the realm's border is marked, in effect, by barking.
